title: Tuberculosis case detection rate (%, all forms)
definition: Tuberculosis case detection rate (all forms) is the number of new and relapse tuberculosis cases notified to WHO in a given year, divided by WHO's estimate of the number of incident tuberculosis cases for the same year, expressed as a percentage. Estimates for all years are recalculated as new information becomes available and techniques are refined, so they may differ from those published previously.

source.license: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International unless specifically labeled otherwise by World Bank metadata
source.sourceOrganization: Global Tuberculosis Report, World Health Organization (WHO)

editorial.patternNote: The darkest countries include Belgium, Croatia, Bulgaria, while the lightest include Mongolia, Djibouti, Eritrea; that contrast is the fastest way to read the tb case detection pattern.
editorial.whyItMatters: TB case detection turns disease surveillance and health access into a challenging but fair map.

bestProbeCountries.reason: High anchor: this country sits near the darkest end of the map.
Low anchor: this country clarifies the lightest end of the scale.
Middle check: this country helps separate a true pattern from one-off outliers.

commonConfusions.reason: Logistics infrastructure quality overlaps with part of the visible pattern, but the full country spread does not match.
Logistics performance overlaps with part of the visible pattern, but the full country spread does not match.
Logistics tracking score overlaps with part of the visible pattern, but the full country spread does not match.

editorial.difficultyReason: Expert difficulty: the map can be mistaken for adjacent indicators unless players probe a high, low, and middle country.
editorial.dataCaveat: This modeled percentage can exceed 100 where notifications surpass estimated incidence.
regionalSignals: Health maps often separate income, public-health capacity, and conflict exposure.
Check both high-income and low-income anchors.

reviewNotes: Batch 4 QA: useful surveillance/access map, but the modeled percentage can be unintuitive.
Expert-only until reveal text handles values over 100 percent clearly.
